prompt: FIXED foreground subject: the woman and everything she is wearing. In the source image she stands on a plain white studio backdrop — remove that backdrop completely and replace it with the new scene described below, but treat HER as a locked, unedited layer. Do NOT regenerate, restyle, relight, or reshape her face, body, hair, skin tone, dress, print, or footwear. Preserve exactly: warm tan skin; long dark-brown centre-parted hair past the shoulders; relaxed expression; arms loose at her sides; a sleeveless ivory/cream tiered cotton midi dress in a tiny pink-and-sage ditsy floral print, scooped neckline with a small self-tie bow at the chest, shirred waist, lace-trim band on the lower tier; a fine gold chain; low pearl-embellished clear sandals. Generate ONLY a new photographic background. Match the new scene's light to her existing soft, even, warm frontal lighting — open shade, gentle and diffused, no harsh backlight, no hard new shadows across her. CRITICAL GROUNDING: render a clearly visible, soft-edged contact shadow pooling directly beneath and just around both sandals, anchoring her firmly to the ground — she must read as planted, never floating. TONAL UNITY: keep the wall, ground and all shadows in the same warm ochre register as the light on her, so subject and scene read as one single exposure with no cool-vs-warm mismatch. BACKGROUND: A pale ochre limewashed wall in soft diffused daylight, with the dappled shadow of olive leaves with a faint warm sunlit patch on the wall behind her falling gently across it from an unseen tree overhead. A rustic weathered-wood bench edge enters low at camera-left, casting its own soft but clearly defined shadow onto the ground consistent with the diffused overhead light. One closed, sun-faded timber shutter sits camera-right. Soft warmth, airy. Warm and textural — personality from patina and leaf-shadow, nothing cluttered. FRAMING: vertical portrait, about 2:3. Full figure centred, filling roughly 88% of frame height, a little ground beneath her sandals and slight headroom above. Eye-level camera, ~35-50mm look, medium depth of field so the background reads clearly but sits softly behind her.
